wikimediacustomizations-attribution-rest:
The Attribution API provides well-structured attribution data for content across Wikimedia projects. For more information about the Attribution API, see the [docs on mediawiki.org](https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Special:MyLanguage/Attribution_API). For more information about attribution guidelines, expectations, and style guides when reusing Wikimedia content, see the [Attribution Framework](https://wikimedia-attribution.toolforge.org/attribution-signals/overview.html).

Have questions or comments about this API? Help shape the experience and functionality while during the beta period by [joining the discussion](https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Talk:Attribution_API).
wikimediacustomizations-attribution-endp: Get attribution information for a page.

Attribution signals are the individual elements that keep Wikimedia projects visible when their content is surfaced in external contexts. Signals provide direct acknowledgment and access to sources, and present additional information that helps end users evaluate and engage with the knowledge they encounter.

Attribution signals may be pulled for any Wikimedia content page, but are most applicable to articles and media files. Not all information will be returned for every type of page; for example, the credit is only returned on media files. To differentiate page type differences from missing data, only fields applicable to the given page type are included in the response, and `null` values are used in cases where data is expected but unavailable.
wikimediacustomizations-attribution-get-: Allows the caller to include additional types of signals in the response, following the [Attribution Framework](https://wikimedia-attribution.toolforge.org/) categories. By default, only `Essential` signals are returned. Additional categories can be specified as a comma-separated list. Available values: `trust_and_relevance`, `calls_to_action`.
Essential attribution signals
Page title
Artist or author of the file
Content license
Link to license details
License name
Page URL
Icons and logos for the wiki
Brand mark name
URL to download the brand mark
Type of brand mark, such as `icon`, `logo`, or `audio`
Details about the wiki
Localized wiki name
Type of wiki, such as `wikipedia` or `wiktionary`
Wiki identifier
Content language of the wiki, formatted as a language code
Content language of the page, formatted as a language code
Trust and relevance signals
Number of contributors
Timestamp of the latest update in ISO 8601 format
Number of page views in the last 30 days
Number of references cited. This property is omitted for file pages and may be `null` if the count cannot be determined.
Calls to action
Calls to action for donation
Default call to action for donation
Call to action for donation to the Wikimedia Foundation
Call to action for donation for a special campaign
Calls to action for participation
Call to action for participation on the talk page
Trending indicators show when Wikipedia articles experienced a higher volume of page views, edits or both. Trending activity often correlates with current events.
Indicates whether a Wikipedia article ranked among the project’s most read, most edited, or both over the last 24 hours
The page is currently in the top 1000 read pages for the wiki project.
The page is currently in the top 100 edited pages for the wiki project, based on number of edits.
The page is currently in the top 1000 read and top 100 edited pages for the wiki project.
Indicates the page is currently outperforming its average read and edit rates.
The page is currently exceeding its average read rate.
The page currently has a higher rate of edits being submitted than its average.
The page currently has more read and edit activity than its average performance.
